title: Identifiable-patient-content detector + fixtures
context: Some published OA cancer articles — especially case reports — embed potentially identifiable patient media (clinical photographs, rare-disease detail, full-face images, supplementary patient-level tables). Redistributing these in a bundle without detection is a serious privacy harm. The gate-002 checklist mandates a patient-content detector; this task implements it. The detector must use only signals from already-public metadata (study type, article structure, media element tags) — it must never attempt to identify, link, re-identify, extract, or enhance identifying content. Flagged articles are kept metadata-only. Depends on gate-002 (detector signal definition) and license-004 (for integration into the pipeline).
objective: Implement the identifiable-patient-content detector: classify articles using the reviewer-approved signal set (case-report study type + clinical-image/full-face/patient-media/individual-level-supplementary signals), route flagged articles to metadata-only, and provide synthetic fixtures that prove flagged cases are blocked in CI.
acceptanceCriteria: Implements the reviewer-approved signal set: case-report study-type classification plus clinical-image, full-face, patient-media, and individual-level-supplementary-data signals derived from already-public article metadata and JATS structure tags.
Flagged articles are set to patientContentFlag {flagged:true, reason:<signal>, action:'metadata-only'} and excluded from all redistributable bundles; fullText.reuseAllowedInBundle is set false.

Ships synthetic fixtures: at least one flagged case (case report with patient-media signal) and at least one clean case; CI asserts the flagged case is forced metadata-only and does not appear in any redistributable bundle.
Never extracts, enhances, links, or re-identifies individuals; only records the signal type and flag reason; no real flagged content (patient images, patient tables) is committed to the repository.

The detector's signal definitions are documented and have been reviewed/approved by the License+compliance reviewer (pmc-cancer-reviewer-014); open questions about sensitivity vs. over-flagging are surfaced and recorded.
Code MIT licensed; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; commit is DCO signed-off.
resources: pmc-cancer-gate-002 (patient-content screen criterion — signals and disposition logic)
PLAN.md (Data, licensing & compliance — Guardrail #2: Identifiable-patient-content screen)
output: A merged PR adding the patient-content detector module (TypeScript/ESM), synthetic fixtures (flagged + clean cases), CI assertions enforcing metadata-only routing, and reviewer-approved signal documentation.
